title: Quantization: Shrinking Numbers
concept: Quantization reduces model size and speeds up inference by converting 32-bit floating-point weights into 8-bit integers.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is a key advantage of edge deployment?

options.text: It runs locally without needing an active internet connection
It allows models of infinite size to run instantly
It speeds up the backpropagation training process

exercises.explain: Because computation happens locally, edge systems function completely offline.

cards.title: Fitting a giant in a suitcase
cards.body: A standard neural network stores its weights as 32-bit decimal numbers. This is like writing down every distance in your room to the exact millimeter. Do we really need that much precision? By rounding these numbers, we can shrink the model size by 75%!

cards.title: What is Quantization?
cards.body: Quantization is the process of mapping continuous 32-bit floating-point values (FP32) to lower-precision representations, typically 8-bit integers (INT8). This reduces memory footprint and accelerates computation.

cards.title: Rounding prices at the store
cards.body: Instead of saying an item costs $9.97, $4.03, and $2.99, you round them to $10, $4, and $3. It makes the math in your head much faster, and the total is still very close to the actual sum!

cards.title: Shrinking a model to 25MB
cards.body: An engineer quantizes a 100MB object detection model from FP32 to INT8. The model size drops to 25MB, allowing it to easily fit within the strict download limit of a mobile app store.

cards.title: Losing decimals doesn't ruin the model
cards.body: You might think that converting floats to integers will make the model blind. Surprisingly, neural networks are highly robust to noise; rounding weights usually results in less than a 1% drop in accuracy.

left.text:
32 bits per number
Highly precise decimals
e.g., 0.12345678
Slow and memory-heavy

right.text:
8 bits per number
Whole numbers (-128 to 127)
e.g., 15
Fast and 4x smaller

exercises.prompt: How many bits are typically used for standard high-precision weights (FP32) versus quantized weights (INT8)?
exercises.explain: Standard floats use 32 bits, while quantized integers use 8 bits, which is 4 times smaller.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Quantizing a model reduces both its storage size on disk and its memory usage during runtime.
exercises.explain: True. Rounding weights to 8 bits reduces file sizes and memory consumption during execution.

exercises.sentence: Converting 32-bit floats to 8-bit integers is known as model ___.
exercises.explain: Model quantization refers to the reduction of numerical precision in model parameters.

exercises.prompt: What is a typical speed benefit of running INT8 math on compatible edge chips?

options.text: INT8 math is faster and uses less CPU/GPU energy
INT8 math is slower but has higher precision
INT8 math runs without using any processor cores

exercises.explain: Most modern edge chips have dedicated integer compute blocks that process 8-bit values very quickly and efficiently.

exercises.prompt: You are deploying an image filter model to a smartphone app. The original model is 200MB and loads slowly in RAM. You need to shrink it with minimal engineering effort. What is the first optimization to try?

options.text: Quantize the model weights from FP32 to INT8.
Rewrite the app in a different programming language.
Retrain the model on 10 times more high-resolution images.
Run the model twice in parallel on the phone.

exercises.explain: Quantization is a quick, post-training step that immediately shrinks the model by 4x (to 50MB) and speeds up execution with minimal accuracy loss.
